about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: Pete Brown, Don Frye and myself. I guess I was there around four or five years... And when I left there, I got an offer to go down to the Brickwood Cafe with the same band.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The Scott Brothers, Don Frye and Dickie Wells had New York experience and I was learning more and more about music... It was a good thing to have two trumpet players in the same orchestra with different styles who worked well together in the ensemble.

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: I had Sidney DeParis, Joe Eldridge and Don Frye... And then Don Frye came in. And that's the outfit we brought over to Jimmy Ryans.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Don Frye, the pianist in that band, and I are very good friends.

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Don Fry is mentioned only as a musician who played at the Metropole club: 'a terrific guy named Fitz Weston. These are all people who are not too well known. Don Fry, who had been at Jimmy Ryans’.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: our original group composed of Lloyd Scott, Don Frye, Luther Watson, McKinney, and later on came Milt Senior... and that was the beginning of the Cotton Pickers.
